entry.title: Re: Publish views from POD's to other users
entry.content:
I also tried it, but there was the issue that first the worklist is loaded with the default settings of the WC/Resource configured in the POD.

Then the URL parameters are set in the filtering but no refresh is done, so this means that you see incorrect data at that time. That is the big difference with the view, if you store the WC/Resource selection in a view and you select that view then also a refresh of the worklist is done.

entry.title: Re: Way to automatically remove roles from users who have...
entry.content:
Hope you are doing well!

There is no such application job in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to remove roles.

However, there is a way to lock unused users as mentioned in the Help Portal Page below:

How to Lock Unused Business Users | SAP Help Portal

You can adjust the parameter and the setting inside for your business requirement.

Thanks and Best Regards,

Bell

entry.title: Synchronous Goods Receipts in EWM decentralized warehouses with MIGO
entry.content:
Scenario: 2 EWM decentralized warehouses mapped under the same ERP plant.  Shipments between warehouses are done daily in both directions. No billing / invoicing involved. 
Request: Research possibility to trigger the movements directly from MIGO tx. (311 movement) in order to avoid the standard INTRACO STO.  Currently they managed both physical warehouses under the same roof at their current WMS. We want to find the easiest setup to avoid this configuration in the future
Comments: 

  • Could we use Synchronous goods receipt in this scenario? My understanding is that we cannot. 
  • Is there any way to trigger the deliveries from MIGO under this scenario?
  • Suggestions and /or experiences for this kind of situations are welcome. 

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: How to download Client Certificate for SAP Business B...
entry.content:
The SAP Business ByDesign certificate can be downloaded without creating a communication arrangement.

  1. In SAP Business ByDesign, go to the Application and User Management work center.
  2. Select Communication Arrangements and choose Download Tenant Certificate.
You can also refer to the below Help Document and KBA:
  • Downloading Client Certificate for SAP Business ByDesign
  • 3307537 - How to Download the Full Chain Certificate From SAP Business ByDesign System to Use in Peppol

entry.title: Re: Couldn't establish a secure connection to SAP BW (fro...
entry.content:
You're seeing a Secure Connection Failed issue when trying to use SNC with AFO SAC Edition for SSO to SAP BW. Since SAP GUI with SNC works, the issue likely lies in the AFO SNC configuration.

Make sure of the following:-

  1. AFO is using the same Secure Login Client and SNC settings as SAP GUI.

  2. The SNC name in the AFO connection matches exactly with the backend.

  3. Ensure the SAP CommonCryptoLib is correctly installed and accessible to AFO.

Once aligned, the secure connection and SSO should work smoothly. Hope it helps!

entry.title: Re: SAC Transport - to overwrite objects, but not data
entry.content:
Dear @Oleks_Master,

to transport master and transaction data, you need to activate Data in the Selected objects view.

 

With this setting, your master and transaction data will be exported.

During import you can select if you want to overwrite content with data (master and transaction data) or content only by removing deactivating the Include Data option.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Best regards
Susanne

entry.title: Re: Fetching Document id's for a Contract Workspace throu...

Hi Riyaz,
You can use the Project Document Management Retrieval API, with the endpoint as /projectDocuments/:ContractId as follows: 

GET https://{{baseUrl}}/api/retrieve-project-document/v1/prod/projectDocuments/CW1234567?
    &user={{user}}
    &passwordAdapter={{passwordAdapter}}
    &realm={{realm}}
accept: application/json
apiKey: {{apiKey}}
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}

Thank you

entry.title: SAP QM: How to Control Result Recording of a Non-Sequential MIC Based on Another MIC's Result
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have a requirement in SAP QM result recording (QE51N / QE01) related to dependent characteristics within the same operation (e.g., 0010).

 Business Logic:

  • MIC 2 is a decision MIC.

  • MIC 9 is dependent on MIC 2’s result.

The expected logic is:

  • If MIC 2 = Accept, then MIC 9 should be skipped

  • If MIC 2 = Reject, then MIC 9 must be recorded(either any other mic in non sequence)

Important Context:

I know that SAP supports sequence-based dependency — where the system controls the result recording of next MICs (like 3, 4) based on previous MIC results using sequence control and inspection points.

However, my requirement is different:

  • MIC 9 is not sequentially after MIC 2

  • MIC 9 may appear later in the list or randomly

  • I want to control MIC 9’s recording (mandatory or skip) based on MIC 2’s result — regardless of sequence

My Questions:

  1. Is there a standard enhancement point (User Exit or BAdI) where I can read the result of MIC 2 and enforce logic on MIC 9?

  2. Can QE_RESULT_PROCESSING (BAdI) or QEEM_RESULT_RECORDING_01 (User Exit) be used for such MIC-level validation during result save?

  3. Will this work for both QE51N and QE01?

  4. Are there any sample codes, implementation guidance, or alternative approaches for this kind of logic?

System Info:

  • All MICs are in the same operation

  • MIC 2 is required

  • MIC 9 is configured as optional + skip allowed

  • No sequence control or separate operations used

Any advice or real-time examples would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Using the SAP Cloud Javascript SDK for AI
entry.content:
Thank you, @aliulashayir for sharing your experience with the SDK 😊 Highly appreciated!

This is Junjie from the Cloud SDK team.

I would like to point out:

1. Normally, you dont have to use the destination, if you have the AI Core service bound to your application. The SDK will read the client id/secret from the VCAP Service environment. (doc) If you dont have the AI Core service bound to your application, e.g., because the AI Core service is in another BTP landscape/region, then creating a destination can solve the problem.

2. You'll find more sample code in our repository here.

entry.title: Google Play requires that apps target API level 35 or higher
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I recently received a notification from Google Play stating that my app must target API level 35 or higher by August 31, 2025, to comply with their new requirements. Currently, my target SDK is set to API level 34.

Here is the message from Google Play 

*Google Play requires that apps target API level 35 or higher.

Update your target API level by August 31, 2024, to publish updates to your app. We have detected that your app targets an older version of Android. To provide users with a secure experience, Google Play requires all apps to meet the target API level requirements.

As of August 31, 2025, if your target API level is more than one year old compared to the latest Android release, you will not be able to update your app.*

Could someone guide me through the process of updating the target API level in SAP Build Apps? 

Thank you,

entry.title: Establishing Inbound Connection from AWS to HANA Cloud via Cloud Connector
entry.content:
We are working on establishing an inbound connection to our SAP HANA Cloud database from an application hosted on an AWS instance. Our goal is to securely connect our application to the HANA DB.

We have already established a successful connection using AWS PrivateLink. However, for specific architectural reasons, we now need to implement this connection using the SAP Cloud Connector.

Here’s what we have accomplished so far:

  1. Cloud Connector Installation: We have successfully installed the SAP Cloud Connector on a dedicated instance.
  2. SAP HANA Client Installation: The SAP HANA Client has been installed on the application server within AWS.
  3. Subaccount Connection: We have connected our BTP subaccount, which contains our SAP HANA Cloud instance, to the Cloud Connector, and it shows as "Connected."
  4. Cloud to On-Premise Mapping: We have configured a "Cloud to On-Premises" mapping in the Cloud Connector.
    • Backend Type: SAP HANA
    • Virtual Host & Port: We have defined a virtual host and port.
    • Internal Host & Port: We have mapped this to the internal hostname and port of our HANA Cloud database.

Despite these steps, we are facing challenges in getting the application on AWS to communicate with the HANA Database through this Cloud Connector setup.

Our question is:

What are the specific next steps or configurations required on the AWS side and within the BTP subaccount to make the HANA Cloud database accessible from our AWS-hosted application via the virtual host and port we defined in the Cloud Connector?

We have reviewed the standard documentation, but we are looking for insights from anyone who has successfully implemented a similar architecture. Any guidance, best practices, or troubleshooting tips would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your help!

entry.title: Re: How to Manage Multi-User Conversation History in a RA...
entry.content:
Hello,

Let me share my thoughts.

First, as you pointed out, it’s hard to keep user-specific chat history solely inside a Flask app deployed on Cloud Foundry. Using external storage such as HANA Cloud or Redis is the sensible approach. If you build with CAP, HANA Cloud is provisioned automatically, so you can define entities like User_ID and a conversation-history table; that’s likely the best way to create a user-level chat application on SAP (a senior colleague said the same).

As for your second question, I don’t have a definite answer right now. LangChain’s ConversationBufferMemory is in-memory by default, so long-term persistence is inherently difficult.

I hope this helps; feel free to reach out if you have any further questions.

entry.title: Re: Rework Order Component Validation
entry.content:
For the Rework order, it should create a rework production version.

In the rework production version, you can choose a new BOM created for rework and also choose its normal BOM. As you requirement, you can choose its normal BOM. And you can choose a new Routing created for rework and also choose its normal Routing.

Then create a production order with this rework production version, and the components will be the same with the BOM, but if you want to add a new components not in the BOM, the system cannot check it as standard function, maybe you should add a enhancement program to check, thank you!

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA 2023 Fully-Activated Appliance: Known Issues
entry.content:
@ste_herrmie i finally made progress installing the appliance (FPS03). What i did:

I installed Suse Enterprise Linux SP15 from scratch without the "SAP"-Module. I upgraded the kernel to the exact same version found in the SAP_Software_Appliance.xml (5.14.21-150500.55.39-default)

I installed the sapconf tool as described in note 1275776 - Linux: Vorbereitung von SLES für SAP-Umgebungen. - SAP for Me

Additionally I installed :

zypper install libgcc_s1 libstdc++6 libatomic1

zypper install insserv

@JoergWolf (thanks for the hint!)

The file deamon.ini could not be edited right away. I choose to recover from the .daemon.ini.swp that was present in /hana/shared/HDB/HDB02/vhcalhdbdb

After editing daemon.ini i deleted .daemon.ini.swp

After all these steps it finally came  past the step "Restoring PSE" and now seems to work so far...<fingers_crossed/>

Best regards,

Sascha

entry.title: Recap: SAP Inside Track Wroclaw 07-JUN-2025: SQL selection patterns in SAP HANA database
entry.content:
A couple of words about me:    

My name is Bogdan Brzozowski and I’m Managing Software Architect working for Capgemini Poland within more than 14 years. My current position encompasses the following responsibilities:

  • Establishing architecture (ERD, UML) for ABAP based custom solutions to be implemented for our customers by our developers.
  • Reverse engineering of the legacy ABAP solutions belonging to our customers to take the full responsibility for their further development and maintenance.
  • Conducting internal trainings for our technical consultants to improve their technical skills.
  • Presentations for external audience to make our company visible as top provider of SAP technology-based solutions.
  • Custom code conversion guidelines – I am responsible for creation and keeping this document up to date.
  • Spreading knowledge about internal Capgemini data migration tools like Syniti or Concert Tool.

 

 

A couple of sentences about the event:  

SAP Inside Track was held again in Wroclaw this year. That time we met on rainy Saturday day (7-JUNE-2025) in the premises of WBS Merito University - well known private university that was event co-organizer (together with Capgemini). We welcomed attendees from 9 countries, who were participating in the sessions conducted in English by 18 presenters. Most of the presentations were running in parallel split between 2 rooms.  Predominant topics shared with the audience were related to the use of genAI in the context of SAP technologies. But there were also few sessions focusing on modern aspects of ABAP programming, like the subject presented in this blog :-).

 

My Session:    

This year I had the opportunity to do a session called “ SQL selection patterns in CDS views examples”. Good practices are worth following and this is why design patterns like singleton, factory, prototype and many others became popular within object-oriented programming languages. The same may partially apply to data retrieval from any relational database. I noticed that there are typical techniques of data selection, that are useful in different projects for the customers from various branches: pharma, retail, automotive, utility supply to name few of them. In this blog I will share those selection patterns using syntax from ABAP Advanced Open SQL, ABAP CDS view entities and SQL script. HANA database is compliant with standard SQL ANSI 2016, so the examples presented in this blog may be useful to retrieve the data from other relational databases, which are compliant with SQL standard.. Below I would like to share main highlights, insights and key takeaways from my presentation.  

Entity Relationship Diagram of the data model used for the code presented later on this blog.

Sales Order header is kept in VBAK table, while its items in VBAP. Additionally all the changes within Sales Order data are recorded in so called Change Documents with 2 main tables: CDHDR as the change header and CDPOS that stores old and new value of a given field. ERD diagram above uses well known crow's foot notation - see more details here.

 

Main highlights and key takeaways :   

 I have presented 2 selection patterns for the performance improvement:

  • exclusion
  •  case vs union all

...plus 2 functional patterns:

  • predecessor and successor
  • data source determination during runtime.

 

Performance pattern: Exclusion

Let's take a look at ERD diagram again. Imagine the requirement to select all Sales Orders items (VBAP table), that do not refer to any material from MARA or they refer to materials of HAWA type only. (MARA-MTART = 'HAWA'). Additionally we are interested in Sales Orders of TA type only (VBAK-AUART = 'TA').

The very first idea is the use of subquery embedded inside NOT EXISTS operator:

SELECT
  vbak~vbeln,
  vbap~posnr,
  vbap~matnr,
  vbap~netwr,
  vbap~waerk
  FROM vbak
  INNER JOIN vbap
  ON vbap~vbeln = vbak~vbeln
  WHERE vbak~auart = @cv_sd_order_type_ta
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT @abap_true FROM mara AS subq
      WHERE subq~matnr = vbap~matnr
        AND subq~lvorm = @abap_false
        AND subq~mtart = @cv_material_type_hawa
  )
  ORDER BY vbak~vbeln, vbap~posnr
  INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @me->mt_so_head_item[].

But subquery has 2 major disadvantages: it consumes a lot of database server resources (which is valid for any database provider: SAP, Oracle, MS, IBM, etc.) and it cannot be used in ABAP CDS view (valid for SAP HANA database only).

The alternative offering much faster data retrieval is LEFT OUTER JOIN:

SELECT
  vbak~vbeln,
  vbap~posnr,
  vbap~matnr,
  vbap~netwr,
  vbap~waerk,
  mara~mtart,
  mara~xchpf
  FROM vbak
  INNER JOIN vbap
  ON vbap~vbeln = vbak~vbeln
  LEFT OUTER JOIN mara
  ON  mara~matnr = vbap~matnr
  AND mara~lvorm = @ABAP_false "NOT in WHERE if OUTER join is expected
  WHERE vbak~auart = @CV_sd_order_type_ta
  AND (    mara~matnr IS NULL
        OR mara~mtart <> @CV_material_type_hawa
      )
   ORDER BY vbak~vbeln, vbap~posnr
  INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @ME->mt_so_head_item[].

ATTENTION: any additional conditions against the data source from the left side of the outer join must be specified within ON operator, not as a part of WHERE clause. Otherwise you will transform your OUTER join (unintentionally) into the INNER join! You may get caught into this trap using Advanced Open SQL (with @ prefix when you refer to the variables declared in ABAP and residing on application server).  Old Open SQL flavor for ABAP programming on ECC system (predecessor of S/4HANA) did not allow such implicit (and most likely unwanted) conversion of OUTER join into the INNER one. 

Association is not part of SQL standard, but it may be perceived like lazy outer join. This 'laziness' means that as long as ABAP app does not require the columns from the data source on the left side of the outer join, the database system does not perform outer join operation at all, saving some processing time and its resources. This is why we can use the similar technique to obtain data with our exclusion condition from the CDS view that uses association:

SELECT
  SalesOrder AS vbeln,
  so~\_Item-SalesOrderItem AS posnr,
  so~\_Item-Product AS matnr,
  so~\_Item-NetAmount AS netwr,
  so~\_Item-TransactionCurrency AS waerk,
  so~\_Item\_Product-ProductType AS mtart,
  so~\_Item\_Product-IsBatchManagementRequired AS xchpf
  FROM R_SalesOrderTP AS so
  WHERE so~SalesOrderType = @cv_sd_order_type_ta
  AND so~\_Item-SalesOrderItem IS NOT NULL
  AND ( so~\_Item\_Product-ProductType IS NULL
        OR (     so~\_Item\_Product-ProductType <> @cv_material_type_hawa
             AND so~\_Item\_Product-IsMarkedForDeletion = @abap_false
           )
      )
  ORDER BY vbeln, posnr
  INTO TABLE @me->mt_so_head_item[].

Performance pattern: Case vs Union all

Imagine the following projection list using CASE operator:

SELECT
  SalesDocument AS vbeln,
  so~\_ItemBasic-SalesDocumentItem AS posnr,
  so~\_ItemBasic-Material AS matnr,
  so~\_ItemBasic-NetAmount AS netwr,
  so~\_ItemBasic-TransactionCurrency AS waerk,
  so~\_ItemBasic\_Material-MaterialType AS mtart,
  so~\_ItemBasic\_Material-IsBatchManagementRequired AS xchpf,
  CASE
    WHEN so~\_ItemBasic\_Material-IsBatchManagementRequired IS NULL
      THEN 'Batch Managment is IRRELEVANT'
    WHEN so~\_ItemBasic\_Material-IsBatchManagementRequired = @abap_true
      THEN 'Batch Managment IS required'
    ELSE 'Batch Managment is NOT required'
  END AS IsBatchMgmtReq
  FROM I_SalesDocumentBasic AS so
  WHERE so~SalesDocumentType = @cv_sd_order_type_ta
  AND so~\_ItemBasic-SalesDocumentItem IS NOT NULL
  AND (   so~\_ItemBasic\_Material-MaterialType <> @cv_material_type_hawa
       OR so~\_ItemBasic\_Material-MaterialType IS NULL
      )
  ORDER BY vbeln, posnr
  INTO TABLE @me->mt_so_descriptive[].

It may be time consuming to execute, as it runs as a single thread on HANA database server. The alternative is to split such single SELECT statement into as many SELECT statements as many CASE conditions are used. All these SELECT statements will be combined with UNION ALL operator, like in the code below:

SELECT
  SalesDocument AS vbeln,
  so~\_ItemBasic-SalesDocumentItem AS posnr,
  so~\_ItemBasic-Material AS matnr,
  so~\_ItemBasic-NetAmount AS netwr,
  so~\_ItemBasic-TransactionCurrency AS waerk,
  so~\_ItemBasic\_Material-MaterialType AS mtart,
  so~\_ItemBasic\_Material-IsBatchManagementRequired AS xchpf,
  'Batch Managment is IRRELEVANT' AS IsBatchMgmtReq
  FROM I_SalesDocumentBasic AS so
  WHERE so~SalesDocumentType = @cv_sd_order_type_ta
  AND so~\_ItemBasic-SalesDocumentItem IS NOT NULL
  AND (    so~\_ItemBasic\_Material-MaterialType <> @cv_material_type_hawa
        OR so~\_ItemBasic\_Material-MaterialType IS NULL
      )
  AND so~\_ItemBasic\_Material-IsBatchManagementRequired IS NULL
UNION ALL
SELECT
  SalesDocument AS vbeln,
  so~\_ItemBasic-SalesDocumentItem AS posnr,
  so~\_ItemBasic-Material AS matnr,
  so~\_ItemBasic-NetAmount AS netwr,
  so~\_ItemBasic-TransactionCurrency AS waerk,
  so~\_ItemBasic\_Material-MaterialType AS mtart,
  so~\_ItemBasic\_Material-IsBatchManagementRequired AS xchpf,
  'Batch Managment IS required' AS IsBatchMgmtReq
  FROM I_SalesDocumentBasic AS so
  WHERE so~SalesDocumentType = @cv_sd_order_type_ta
  AND so~\_ItemBasic-SalesDocumentItem IS NOT NULL
  AND (    so~\_ItemBasic\_Material-MaterialType <> @cv_material_type_hawa
        OR so~\_ItemBasic\_Material-MaterialType IS NULL
      )
  AND so~\_ItemBasic\_Material-IsBatchManagementRequired = @abap_true
UNION ALL
SELECT
  SalesDocument AS vbeln,
  so~\_ItemBasic-SalesDocumentItem AS posnr,
  so~\_ItemBasic-Material AS matnr,
  so~\_ItemBasic-NetAmount AS netwr,
  so~\_ItemBasic-TransactionCurrency AS waerk,
  so~\_ItemBasic\_Material-MaterialType AS mtart,
  so~\_ItemBasic\_Material-IsBatchManagementRequired AS xchpf,
  'Batch Managment is NOT required' AS IsBatchMgmtReq
  FROM I_SalesDocumentBasic AS so
  WHERE so~SalesDocumentType = @cv_sd_order_type_ta
  AND so~\_ItemBasic-SalesDocumentItem IS NOT NULL
  AND (    so~\_ItemBasic\_Material-MaterialType <> @cv_material_type_hawa
        OR so~\_ItemBasic\_Material-MaterialType IS NULL
      )
  AND so~\_ItemBasic\_Material-IsBatchManagementRequired = @abap_false
  ORDER BY vbeln, posnr
INTO TABLE @me->mt_so_descriptive[].

Each of 3 SELECT commands above contains respective conditions from the CASE operator used on the projection list of the single SELECT instruction. Such approach allows database system execute 3 separate threads in parallel and then combine their results via UNION ALL.  

ATTENTION: do not use UNION without ALL keyword, because the advantage of parallel processing may be overshadowed with additional effort to eliminate duplicates from the result set (mt_so_descriptive[] internal table in our example). 

 

Functional pattern: predecessor and successor

Imagine the situation in which we need to provide old and new values of the given table field in chronological order. 2 main tables of Change Documents (CDHDR - header and CDPOS - position) store such information. Our goal is to retrieve the pairs of the values for a given field - the old value before the change (predecessor) and the new value saved as the result of the change (successor). Subquery seems to be inevitable option to achieve this functionality, so SQL script implemented in ABAP Managed Database Procedure (AMDP) global class is the way to go. But first let's prepare CDS view that joins CDHDR and CDPOS before programming in SQL script:

define view entity Z_I_CD
with parameters
  iv_objclass : cdobjectcl,
  iv_tabname : tabname,
  iv_fldname : fieldname
as select from cdhdr as hdr
inner join cdpos as pos
on  pos.objectid = hdr.objectid
and pos.changenr = hdr.changenr
{
  key hdr.objectid,
  key hdr.changenr,
  key pos.tabkey,
  key pos.chngind,
  hdr.username,
  concat(hdr.udate, hdr.utime) as changed_on,
  pos.unit_old,
  pos.unit_new,
  pos.cuky_old,
  pos.cuky_new,
  pos.value_old,
  pos.value_new
}
where hdr.objectclas = $parameters.iv_objclass
and pos.tabname = $parameters.iv_tabname
and pos.fname = $parameters.iv_fldname

Another good practice used in the implementation above is the use of CDS view parameters: iv_objclass, iv_tabname and iv_fldname as we need to track the changes for the specific table field only. Concatenation of the date and time indicating when exactly the change occurred is crucial to build appropriate subquery in SQL script - see the field CHANGED_ON:

CLASS zcl_amdp_sel_pattern DEFINITION
  PUBLIC FINAL CREATE PUBLIC .

PUBLIC SECTION.
  INTERFACES : if_amdp_marker_hdb.

  CLASS-METHODS:
    read_cd_pair FOR TABLE FUNCTION z_f_cd_pair,
    read_toax FOR TABLE FUNCTION z_f_toax.

  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_amdp_sel_pattern IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD read_cd_pair
    BY DATABASE FUNCTION
    FOR HDB LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT
    OPTIONS READ-ONLY
    USING z_i_cd.
    lt_result =
    select
      cd.mandt,
      cd.objectid,
      cd.tabkey,
      cd.changenr,
      cd.chngind,
      cd.username,
      cd.changed_on,
      cd.unit_old,
      cd.unit_new,
      cd.cuky_old,
      cd.cuky_new,
      cd.value_old,
      cd.value_new,
      pred.username as pred_username,
      pred.changed_on as pred_changed_on,
      pred.unit_old as pred_unit_old,
      pred.unit_new as pred_unit_new,
      pred.cuky_old as pred_cuky_old,
      pred.cuky_new as pred_cuky_new,
      pred.value_old as pred_value_old,
      pred.value_new pred_value_new
      from z_i_cd(
        iv_objclass => :iv_objclass,
        iv_tabname => :iv_tabname,
        iv_fldname => :iv_fldname ) as cd
      left outer join z_i_cd(
        iv_objclass => :iv_objclass,
        iv_tabname => :iv_tabname,
        iv_fldname => :iv_fldname ) as pred
      on pred.mandt = cd.mandt
      and pred.objectid = cd.objectid
      and pred.tabkey = cd.tabkey
      and pred.changed_on = (
        select MAX( cdlast.changed_on )
          from z_i_cd(
            iv_objclass => :iv_objclass,
            iv_tabname => :iv_tabname,
            iv_fldname => :iv_fldname ) as cdlast
          where cdlast.objectid = cd.objectid
          and cdlast.tabkey = cd.tabkey
          and cdlast.changed_on < cd.changed_on );
    RETURN :lt_result;
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

Optional predecessor (see alias PRED for Z_I_CD in outer join above) must have  the greatest available value of the field CHANGED_ON but still lower than CHANGED_ON in successor entry (alias CD). Code available in github.com contains testing console class  ZCL_SEL_PATTERN_TEST_CONSOLE that retrieves the pair of predecessor and successor with regard to the changes in VBAP-MATNR field (material of the Sales Order position).

 

Functional pattern: dynamic data source determination in CDS view

Dynamic data source determination in ABAP is a trivial task to implement:

 
DATA lt_toa01 TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF toa01.
DATA(lv_table_name) = 'TOA01'.

SELECT * FROM (lv_table_name) INTO TABLE @lt_toa01[].

Archive link tables like TOA01, TOA02, TOA03, etc. share identical structure, but based on the configuration each of these tables stores the links for the documents of different SAP objects. Implementing a similar solution in CDS view is not such straightforward topic. It is possible only via table function: 

@EndUserText.label: 'Any TOA0x table'
define table function Z_F_TOAX
with parameters 
  iv_table_name : tabname16
returns {
  mandt : abap.clnt;
  sap_object : saeanwdid;
  object_id : saeobjid;
  archiv_id : saearchivi;
  arc_doc_id : saeardoid;
  ar_object : saeobjart;
  ar_date : saeabadate;
  del_date : saedeldate;
  reserve : saereserve;
}
implemented by method zcl_amdp_sel_pattern=>read_toax;

...and AMDP class:

METHOD read_toax
  BY DATABASE FUNCTION
  FOR HDB LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT
  OPTIONS READ-ONLY
  USING toa01.

  DECLARE lv_query STRING;
  DECLARE lt_result TABLE LIKE toa01;

  lv_query = 'SELECT * FROM ' || :iv_table_name;

  EXECUTE IMMEDIATE lv_query INTO lt_result;

  RETURN :lt_result;
ENDMETHOD.

As we can see the whole trick is the use of EXECUTE IMMEDIATE command that SQL script offers. The source of data selection is delivered into AMDP method as input parameter iv_table_name.

   

Recommended resources for your readers to continue with your learning journey:  

Working code presented in this blog can be downloaded from here:

https://github.com/brbogdan/SQL-selection-patterns-in-S4

 

Related community managed tags you should subscribe to remain informed about this topic:    

ABAP Development

SQL

  

Related community blog posts:    

CDS Views Vs Traditional ABAP Logic Performance

SQL Scripts in SAP HANA

 

 How was the event for you?:    

 My session  has gathered around 40 participants, who were acting interactively despite being a bit exhausted after big portion of the preceding sessions and rainy weather :-).  I hope that some of them will have a chance to implement the patterns I shared on their commercial projects for their real customers. 

I have also benefited from this event taking part in some very impressive sessions:

  • Level up your CAP skills by learning how to use the cds REPL presented by DJ Adams @qmacro 
  • AI agents collaboration for SAP business processes using Google A2A protocol by Igor Narodowy 
  • Why is it becoming increasingly difficult to keep up with SAP? by Paweł Pawlak @PawelPawlak
  • AI agents and SAP. What's the buzz? by Vitaliy Rudnytskiy @Vitaliy-R 
  • 3D modeling in UI5 by Nico Schoenteich @nicoschoenteich 
  • SAPdalf Crafts AI agents by Łukasz Skorwider
  • SAP migrations and GenAI: pioneering the path forward by Tomasz Wilk

Many thanks to these speakers from me!     

 

Now let’s hear from you:    

Before ending the blog post I want to share that one of the things I enjoyed the most in SAP Inside Track Wroclaw 2025. It was meeting new people and old colleagues I haven't seen for a while . Who said this kind of fun needs to end in SAP Inside Track?:) Please introduce yourself in the comment’s section and share what you do in the world of SAP?

entry.title: Master Your Customer Data with SAP Customer Data Cloud: Data Schema Deep Dive!
entry.content:
Do you want to truly harness the power of your customer data? Stop just collecting data and start using it to its fullest potential! Join our upcoming live session, Unlocking the Potential of SAP Customer Data Cloud: Mastering Data Schema, and become a customer data master!

This isn't just another webinar; it's your chance to get hands-on experience configuring and designing a robust data schema within SAP Customer Data Cloud (CDC). We'll demystify data stores, user account structures, schema fields, and group models, empowering you to optimize your customer data management.

What you'll learn

  • Choose the right data store: We'll guide you through selecting the perfect data store for your specific requirements.
  • Master user account structures: Learn how to effectively utilize user account structures for optimal data organization.
  • Confidently manage schema fields: Gain the skills to view and edit schema fields with ease.
  • Design efficient group & relationship models: Discover how to create cohesive and efficient models for streamlined data management.

  • Build a tailored data store schema: Put your knowledge into practice and design a data store schema tailored to your organization's unique needs.

Who should attend?

This session is perfect for:

  • Business Users
  • Technical Consultants
  • Functional Consultants
  • Administrators

Ready to unlock the potential of your customer data?

Register now and join us live on August 20th at 15:00 ~ 16:00 SGT (GMT+8)!

entry.title: Joule in the SAP BTP Cockpit is Now Available for all Customer Accounts!
entry.content:
The day has finally arrived: Joule in the SAP BTP Cockpit is now available in all customer and partner global accounts. Joule began to make its appearance in select global accounts starting in April 2025. Following a staged roll out, we are happy to announce that all customer and partner global accounts with SAP default identity provider access can now access Joule in the cockpit.

Jump in with Joule in the Cockpit

Before you can see the Joule icon in the cockpit tool bar, a global account administrator first needs to enable Joule. By navigating to the Global Account Settings and selecting Joule, an administrator accepts the terms and conditions and toggles the button Enable Joule. Now all account users will be able to see the Joule icon and get started with Joule.

One thing to remember, you must access the cockpit from the SAP default identity provider.

 

What Can Joule Do for Me?

You can ask Joule to perform a pre-defined set of administrative tasks on your behalf. Want to know what Joule can do? Just ask!

It's important to remember that Joule is bound to your cockpit authorization. In other words, Joule can only accomplish tasks for you that you're also authorized to perform, which may not span the entire range of Joule’s capabilities.

 

 

For example, Joule can provide you with information about your accounts and their users:

 

Have a question about the cockpit or the BTP? Joule’s got you covered. Just type a question in the Joule chat using natural language and let Joule scan the product documentation on the SAP Help Portal for answers. Joule summarizes the relevant SAP Help Portal information. This summary is then displayed along with the top help results. Clicking on the link will take you directly to the topic on the SAP Help Portal.

See Supported Administrative Tasks with Joule in the Cockpit to learn more about what Joule can do for you.

 

Get the Most out of Joule

New to Joule? We’ve got you covered with these helpful tips to get you started:

Start Fresh Conversations: Feel free to start a new conversation whenever you like. Just select the “New Conversation” button in the Joule menu.

Hide and Resume Chats: You can hide the Joule chat window by selecting the "X" and pick up right where you left off by selecting the Joule icon in the cockpit tool header.

Conversation Expiry: Conversations expire after 8 hours. Joule will notify you when this happens and offer the option to start a new conversation.

Single Requests Work Best: Joule in BTP Cockpit responds best to single requests. For example, if you want to see users in both your global account and a specific subaccount, it’s better to send these requests separately.

Use Suggested Actions: Sometimes selecting a suggested action or option can provide better results than typing.

Add Context for Better Answers: Providing additional context can help Joule give you more accurate answers. For instance, if you want to see users of a specific subaccount, Joule may ask for the subaccount ID or name.

 

Your Feedback Makes Joule Better

Already had a chance to check out Joule in the cockpit? We’d love to hear from you! Simply tell Joule you’d like to give feedback. Your valuable feedback helps us improve the user experience.

You can also quickly rate Joule’s responses at any time by using the thumbs-up/thumbs-down option.

 

Stay Up to Date

Joule in the cockpit is just getting started! We continue to add new scenarios to Joule, so make sure you stay up to date by checking out What’s New for Joule in the BTP Cockpit on the SAP Help Portal.

For more information about working with Joule in the cockpit, check out Account Administration Using Joule in the Cockpit.

entry.title: Queries regarding Retrieve Footprint (Corporate and Products) API
entry.content:
1. What types of emissions are included in the current API called "Retrieve Footprint (Corporate and Products)", and does this also cover transport-related emissions?
  2. Is it possible to extract emissions data for each stage of the product life cycle, such as procurement, production, and transport?

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Queries regarding Retrieve Footprint (Corporate and P...
entry.content:
It is advised that you no longer use the API "Retrieve Footprint (Corporate and Products)" as this is an old API which has been ear-marked for deprecation in near future.  Before deprecation, you will be notified and given sufficient time to transition. However, we would already strongly encourage customers to move away from this API, and start using the Item Footprints API to be proactive. 

 

The API "Item Footprints" contain the same functionality with more additional features. You can find more about this API here - Overview | Item Footprints | SAP Business Accelerator HuB

With regards to being query on extracting emissions data for each stage of the product life cycle, we do not have a product life cycle stage based separation in the APIs. However, a comparable version exists that is based on 'Item Type' field. So, for example, the ItemFootprints API can be used to access different item-types like 'product', 'production', 'business activity' etc.

 

If you want to know more about the data model of the API, we would recommend you open the scheme details of each API specifically, where an example along with description and explanation of each field is maintained.  You will find these here - SAP Sustainability Footprint Management - Item Footprints - API Reference.

If you check each of the GET API for Item Footprints, it will give you a description of the get request, parameters used, responses which shows an example value and Schema view.

entry.title: Re: Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - A Comprehensive Setup Guide
entry.content:
Hi @MauricioMiao ,

There might be multiple reasons like:

  • Check the workzone->Channel manager -> Content provider -> Edit-> Check if the runtime destination for dynamic data has S/4HANA_PCE_SSO destination. 
  • Check if all the sales order relevant OData APIs are active from. https://me.sap.com/notes/3523238 can be checked to get the relevant OData -  please check the attachments. 
  • Check the cloud connector monitoring to see, if API calls are hitting the S/4. If so, you can check the principal propagation setup or /iwfind/error_log
  • If all of the above troubleshooting fails, you need to create an incident under CA-JOULE

Best regards,

Samir

entry.title: Re: What is this feature of ALV called? The ability to display a heading to every group after sortin
entry.content:
Thank you @Sandra_Rossi .

This has worked. I also searched and managed to find out this thread where you have explained how to get it working completely in front end. And this helped too. -> https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-discussions/alv-report-subtotal-custom-text-in-oo-alv/m-p/556899/highlight/true#M21306

Logging a few of my learnings here, for my own future reference or for others

  1. The feature is called 'Subtotal Text'/'ALV Subtotal Text' 
  2. There MUST be something (a numeric field?) on the ALV we are totaling and something to subtotal/group it under 
    1. In my case, i added a dummy field with 0 value
      1. set NO_ZERO in field catalog to 'X'
      2. set DO_SUM in field catalog to 'X'
    2. I ensured the Subtotal happens at PO Level
      1. I pass in IT_SORT when calling SET_TABLE_FOR_FIRST_DISPLAY
      2. In IT_SORT i have maintained the PO field is added, with SUBTOT = 'X'
      3. In the fieldcatalog for the PO field i ensured NO_OUT = 'X'
  3. I followed BCALV_TEST_GRID_EVENTS to subscribe to the SUBTOTAL_TEXT event
  4. MOST IMPORTANT
    1. I ensured the DUMMY field is at the END, as per field catalog positioning
    2. I originally had this as the first field. And this COMPLETELY PREVENTED the event from triggering (and even the default subtotal text was suppressed). I believe it is because the position of the subtotal value conflicted with the position of the text

EDIT: Not sure how to mark this as answered/Solved. So, if anyone can help, please help.

entry.title: Unlocking Business Potential: The Benefits of Becoming Certified in SAP CX Solutions
entry.content:
In this fast-paced digital economy, customer experience (CX) leads as a critical differentiator for businesses. Becoming certified in SAP CX solutions can significantly enhance your professional value and open up a world of opportunities. Here are some compelling benefits of achieving certification:

Professional Growth and Skill Development

Beyond the immediate benefits to an organisation, becoming certified enhances an individual's career prospects. The certification demonstrates a commitment to continuous learning and professional development, making certified professionals more attractive to employers. As businesses execute on digital transformation strategies, the demand for experts remains, offering ample opportunities for career growth and advancement. An SAP certification can make your resume stand out and position you for advancement within your organisation or potential job moves.

Deep Expertise and Credibility

By becoming certified, you gain a comprehensive understanding of the SAP CX Solutions and how they integrate as part of the SAP Business Suite, to deliver exceptional customer experiences. This depth of knowledge contributes credibility and highlights you as an expert in the field, making you an invaluable asset to any team.

Increased Productivity and Efficiency

With certification comes a deep understanding and mastery of solution-specific skills and tools, which enables you to work more efficiently and effectively. Certified professionals often have access to best practices that can streamline workflows and reduce errors. Additionally, the confidence gained from being certified can boost motivation and focus, leading to higher quality work and faster completion of tasks. Furthermore, certification can open doors to specialised roles and responsibilities, allowing you to tackle more challenging projects that align with your expertise, thus maximising your productivity.

Better Customer Satisfaction

Certification demonstrates a commitment to quality and adherence to recognised industry standards, assuring customers that they are receiving products or services that meet or exceed established benchmarks. Certification also signifies that professionals have undergone rigorous training and assessments, leading to higher expertise and competency. This results in more efficient problem-solving and better overall service.

Innovate Faster

SAP is continually innovating and updating its CX solutions to incorporate the very latest technical advancements, such as AI and data management. Getting certified ensures that you are up-to-date with the latest trends and solutions in customer experience management. This forward-looking approach keeps you relevant and adaptable in a rapidly evolving technological landscape.  The credibility gained from certification can encourage others to support and collaborate on your innovative projects, leading to more successful implementation

Conclusion

Being certified in SAP CX solutions is not just about acquiring a badge; it's about gaining the knowledge and skills to transform how your organisation interacts with customers and manages its operations. By leaning in to continuous learning and driving innovations faster, certification empowers professionals to drive significant improvements in productivity and efficiency. In today's competitive market, this can be the differentiator that sets you and your organisation apart.

Certification is an investment that pays off in enhanced performance, greater customer satisfaction, and a more competitive edge in the ecosystem.

So, why wait? Take the first step towards becoming a certified SAP CX expert and unlock your full potential today.  Visit the SAP Learning site to review the available certifications, including the new 'Get Started' CX Business Certification, designed to be completed in a matter of a few hours - Positioning SAP Business Suite via SAP Customer Experience Solutions.

Matt Duncan

entry.title: Adjusting Reconciliation Account in Asset Accounting – Options in SAP Public Cloud?
entry.content:
Dear community,
We have a problem with a customer who has only now, three months after going live, noticed that something is not quite right in the asset account determination and that inventory postings have therefore been posted to the wrong reconciliation account. They now want to post the entry to a different reconciliation account. In the private cloud, this can be done via the OAMK by specifying that the account is no longer a reconciliation account, and then the transfer posting can be carried out. However, as far as I know, there is no way to do this in the public cloud. How could the asset account determination be assigned to the other reconciliation account? The previous one has already been posted and cannot be changed in theory. 
Does anyone have any ideas or tips?
I would be very grateful.
Many thanks and best regards,
Vanessa

entry.title: Re: how to compare security roles between users
entry.content:
Hi,

One should be able to deduct which roles are assigned to the users. Later compare the authorization objects and values in the roles shared by the users. This is indeed possible like you stated, when using transaction SUIM and its option to evaluate roles. 

In case you're running on a BTP ABAP environment you could aswell utilize the Business Roles Comparison. 

If you want to compare two users there is transaction S_BCE_68001430 to compare assigned authorizations between two user. 

Best regards!

Check 

  • Customizing:  Financial Accounting > General Ledger Accounting > Business Transactions > Open Item Clearing > Prepare Automatic Clearing.
  • If the GR/IR are already defined there, consider scheduling a periodic job for their clearing.
  • 2050268 - MR11/F.13: GR/IR clearing process - SAP ERP & SAP S/4HANA
  • 1156325 - BAdIs in the Logistics Invoice Verification environment

entry.title: Hosting Experience - Fireside Chat with AWS Leaders
entry.content:
SAP Women in Tech (WiT) proudly hosted a Panel Discussion with Nahia Orduna and Chandra Balani on June 11, 2025. It was an evening charged with inspiration, insight, and the incredible energy of a room full of brilliant women in technology. I had the distinct pleasure of co-hosting a recent SAP Women in Tech panel discussion alongside my wonderful colleague, Sthuthi. Seeing so many enthusiastic and like-minded individuals gathered was a powerful reminder of the vibrant community we are building.

The session began on a high note, with a warm welcome from Swetha Mohanty, Head of HR at SAP, who set the tone for the impactful dialogue that followed. 

The panel discussion was graced by two inspiring leaders:

  • Nahia Orduna – Technical Leader, Strategic Accounts at Amazon Web Services (AWS)
  • Chandra Balani – Head of AWS Industries, India

As Sthuthi and I kicked off the evening, the atmosphere was electric. We were honored to be part of an employee-driven community dedicated to furthering SAP's commitment to diversity and inclusion. Our goal for the event was to celebrate the remarkable contributions of women in technology and to recognize the invaluable support of our male allies who champion a more inclusive industry.

 A Journey of Reinvention — Nahia Orduna

Nahia Orduna took us on an inspiring journey through her transformation — from her early days as a lecturer, transitioning into engineering, and ultimately rising to a global leadership position at AWS. 

She shared powerful insights from her book, Your Digital Reinvention, touching on themes of strategic leadership, innovation at scale, and the art of empowering global teams. Her reflections offered a refreshing blend of experience and humility, and her thoughts on integrating teaching with leadership brought in an entirely new dimension of thought. 

What struck a chord with many of us — especially as young professionals — was her relatable, honest advice for women navigating the tech landscape. Her story was not just motivational; it was deeply empowering and gave us several takeaways that we’ll carry forward in our own journey. 

Leading with Impact — Chandra Balani

Following Nahia, Chandra Balani shared equally inspiring stories — especially from the “Her Leadership” series that highlights transformative journeys of women leaders. He also spotlighted several AWS programs focused on developing future-ready leaders, sparking conversations around opportunity, equity, and inclusive growth in tech. 

His words reminded us that allyship and structural support are essential for nurturing women in leadership. His advocacy for women in technology and his storytelling left a lasting impression on all of us. 

More Than a Host: Our Ringside Seat to an Evening of True Inspiration

Walking onto the stage, script in hand, and seeing a room filled with so many bright, inspiring women is a feeling we’ll never forget. With my 13 years in Quality at SAP, I’ve seen our industry evolve, and standing there beside Sthuthi, a Developer at the start of her own promising journey, felt like a perfect representation of the community we were celebrating: a blend of experience and fresh perspectives, united in our passion for technology.

The event was part of SAP's commitment to becoming the most inclusive employer in the world, a goal our employee-driven community passionately supports. Our mission for the evening was not only to celebrate the incredible women driving innovation but also to recognize the vital role of male colleagues who actively champion inclusivity.

Throughout the discussion, the dynamic between the questions Sthuthi and I asked and the insights from Nahia and Chandra was electric. We delved into how to keep pace with innovation, the creative drivers behind Nahia's book "Your Digital Reinvention", and the specific AWS programs our WIT community could leverage. My final question to Nahia was about the valuable lessons she’s learned that she wanted to share with other women in tech. Her closing thoughts were the perfect, empowering endnote before we opened the floor to the audience for a Q&A.

The formal discussion flowed into an informal high tea, a chance to relax and connect. This was where the magic of the evening solidified. The conversations that started on stage continued, new connections were forged, and the sense of community was palpable. This event was more than just a panel discussion; it was an energizing, inspiring, and truly memorable experience that underscored the power of our collective voice.

 

 

 

Final Thought 

To anyone starting their journey in tech, especially women: “Your story matters”. Keep learning, keep leading, and never underestimate the power of your voice.

entry.title: Re: Scrollbars in Grouptree are not working in SP38
entry.content: Hi, Issue exists only when "ToolPanelView" property of Asp.Net CrystalReportViewer is set to None. By default, the GroupTree is hidden. However, when I click the Toggle Group Tree button, the Group Tree is displayed, but the last record or horizontal scroll bar is missing. Here are the specific scenarios: If any record's length exceeds the Group Tree's width, the last record is visible, but the horizontal scroll bar is missing. If all records in the Group Tree are shorter than the Group Tree's width, the last record is missing.
